stylePrompt: Create a completely original lighting product that unifies Biophilic Design with Modern Zen into one coherent contemporary language. Translate natural growth, leaf venation, petals, branches, filtered daylight, and living ecosystems into restrained structure, disciplined negative space, quiet asymmetry, and contemplative light. The lamp should feel alive but never busy, Eastern in spirit but never symbolic, and calming without becoming plain. Use renewable tactile materials, subtle botanical geometry, concealed illumination, and carefully balanced emptiness. The final product must feel restorative, poetic, refined, manufacturable, and suitable for premium modern interiors. Do not make a literal plant-shaped lamp and do not simply place botanical decoration onto a Zen object.
specificElements: A leaf-vein structure reduced to a calm linear frame
A magnolia-petal diffuser balanced by generous empty space
A slender branch-like support translated into precise manufacturable geometry
Layered paper or glass planes filtering light like a quiet tree canopy
A concealed light source washing softly across stone or timber
An asymmetrical seed-pod volume balanced by a low horizontal base
Perforations casting restrained foliage-like shadows onto a plain wall
A calla-lily curve simplified into one continuous structural gesture
A floating luminous plane inspired by paper screens and filtered daylight
The contrast between living organic rhythm and meditative architectural stillness
avoid: Directly combining unrelated Zen and plant motifs
